Japanese: ９月１３日　午前　９時３６分 地方裁判所　被告人第４控え室
English: October 13, 9:36 AM District Court Defendant Lobby No. 4

Japanese: なっ、なるほどくん！
English: Hey, Nick!

Japanese: どうした？　何かあったの‥‥？
English: What is it? Is something wrong...?

Japanese: スゴい人気だよ、今日の法廷！
English: Nah. But did you see all the people here? It's crazy!

Japanese: 見て、見て！　買っちゃったよ、 怪人☆仮面マスクのブロマイド。
English: Oh! Look at this! I bought this Mask☆DeMasque publicity photo!

Japanese: 買った、って‥‥どこで？
English: You bought that...? Where!?

Japanese: 裁判所の前に、 屋台がたくさん出ていますよ！
English: From the little tents in front of the courthouse. They have all sorts of things for sale!

Japanese: あたし、ヨワいんだよねー。 お祭りさわぎ。
English: You know I'm a sucker for this kind of stuff!

Japanese: 真宵ちゃんの《ブロマイド》を 法廷記録に挟んだ。
English: Mask☆DeMasque Publicity Photo stuck onto the Court Record.

Japanese: もお、なんでもいいから、 有罪にしてくださーーーい！
English: Come on! I'm guilty! Throw the book at meeeee!

Japanese: ‥‥あの、キヌを裂くような 殿方のヒメイは‥‥
English: ...Who's screaming like that?

Japanese: ‥‥あ！　成歩堂さん！ いいところに！
English: ...Oh! Mr. Wright! You made it!

Japanese: なんか、マズいところに 来ちゃったみたいですね‥‥
English: Yeah, I did. But it doesn't look like things are going to get any less ugly for you...

Japanese: ボク、やっぱりアレなんです！ ハンニンだったんですぅぅぅッ！
English: Because I did it!! I'm the criminal! Me! Me! Meeeeeee!

Japanese: うううう‥‥また始まったよ。
English: Ungh... He's at it again.

Japanese: 高菱屋に予告状を出して‥‥ やっちゃいましたーーーっ！
English: I sent the calling card to Lordly Tailor! I admit iiiit!!

Japanese: でも‥‥カンジンのツボは 持ってないんですよね？
English: But... You don't have the Sacred Urn, right?

Japanese: まあ、そうなんですけど‥‥
English: Well, that's true, but...

Japanese: それはそれ、これはこれ じゃないですか。
English: That doesn't mean that I didn't commit the crime.

Japanese: （そんなコトないだろう！）
English: (Normally, when I say "Of course you didn't" I'm being sarcastic, but you... Yikes!)

Japanese: とにかく、ボク。 ワルモノなワケですから‥‥
English: Anyway, I admit that I'm guilty...

Japanese: 今日の裁判、 有罪にしてくださーーーい！
English: So make sure they give me a guilty verdict! Pleeeeease!?

Japanese: あ、ユーサクくうん！ グッモオニン！
English: Oh, there you are Ronnie! Bonjour, sweetie!

Japanese: あ！　まま、まれかちゃん！ グッモオニィィン！　‥‥‥いや。
English: Oh! D-Dessie honey! B-Bonjour! ... Well, actually...

Japanese: どうなんだろ、ここで わざわざ英語っていうのもなんか
English: I don't really know why I should be speaking French to you at a time like this

Japanese: ユーサクくん、アタシにまかせて！ きっと、守ったげるからさ！
English: Leave it all to me, Ronnie! I swear I'll protect you!

Japanese: あ‥‥う、うん‥‥でも。 ボク、なんていうかあの。怪人なの
English: Ummm, errr... Aaah... Well, ahh... You see... Actually the thief is err, me

Japanese: いい？　リューイチくん。
English: Can I tell you something, Nicky boy?

Japanese: ユーサクくんの無実は、 このアタシが保証するよ！
English: I can guarantee that my Ronnie is innocent!

Japanese: 有罪になんかしたら、 ひきコロしちゃうから！
English: If he's declared guilty, I'll be ever so cross with you!

Japanese: （にこやかに言われても‥‥）
English: (So why are you smiling when you say it?)

Japanese: じゃ！　アタシ、 ちょっと行くところがあるから。
English: Well, if you'll excuse me! I've got some errands I need to take care of!

Japanese: よろしくね、リューイチくん！
English: I'm counting on you, Nicky boy! Good luck!

Japanese: （天杉　優作が仮面マスクなのか ‥‥正直なところ、わからない）
English: (To be honest, I really don't know whether Ron is Mask☆DeMasque or not.)

Japanese: （たしかなコトは、ただ１つ）
English: (But there's only one thing I AM sure of.)

Japanese: （彼は今、《倉院のツボ》を 持っていない、ということ‥‥）
English: (He doesn't have the Sacred Urn right now...)

Japanese: 被告人！　そろそろ入廷しなさい！
English: Mr. DeLite! It's time for you to enter the courtroom!

Japanese: （今は‥‥まれかさんを 信じるしかない、な‥‥）
English: (For the time being, I guess I'll have to trust Desirée...)

Japanese: 同日　午前１０時 地方裁判所　第６法廷
English: October 13, 10:00 AM District Court Courtroom No. 6

Japanese: これより、天杉　優作の 法廷を開廷します。
English: Court is now in session for the trial of Mr. Ron DeLite.

Japanese: 弁護側、準備完了しています。
English: The defense is ready, Your Honor.

Japanese: 検察側は？　ジュンビのほうは‥‥
English: What about the prosecution? Are you prepared to...

Japanese: ‥‥くだらねえ。
English: ...What a stupid question!

Japanese: なな、なんですか！
English: Wh-What did you say!

Japanese: じゃあ、オレから聞こう、裁判長。 アンタはどうなんだ？
English: Fine. Let me ask you then, Your Honor. Are YOU ready?

Japanese: ‥‥できてるのかい？ 判決のジュンビは。
English: Are you ready to pass judgment?

Japanese: え！　い、いやいや！
English: Wh...! N-No, I... I'm not!

Japanese: 私は、あなたたちのハナシを 聞いてから決めます！
English: I will pass judgment after I hear arguments from both sides.

Japanese: ‥‥自分にできてないコトを ヒトに聞いちゃ、いけねえな。
English: Well, if you're not ready yourself, you shouldn't expect others to be.

Japanese: そいつが‥‥オレのルールだぜ。
English: That's a rule to live by.

Japanese: ‥‥あの。あなたは‥‥？
English: ...Um, wh-who are you?

Japanese: ゴドー。 ‥‥完全無敗、伝説の検事だ。
English: I am Godot. Legendary prosecutor. I've never lost a case.

Japanese: あ！　きのう“あいや！”の 探偵さんが言ってた‥‥
English: Ah! He's the one that Detective Atmey was talking about...

Japanese: ‥‥私も、なぜかウワサだけは 聞いたコトがあります。
English: Yes, your reputation precedes you.

Japanese: 今まで、どのような事件を あつかったのですかな？
English: What kind of cases have you dealt with so far?

Japanese: クッ‥‥！ そんなもの、ねえさ。
English: Ha...! None.

Japanese: はい‥‥？
English: What did you say...?

Japanese: 検事席に立つのは、 今日が初めてだぜ。
English: I've never prosecuted a case before.

Japanese: は、初めて？ さきほど“完全無敗”だと‥‥
English: N-Never? But you said you've never lost before.

Japanese: ‥‥どんな検事だって、 最初は無敗さ‥‥
English: ...Exactly. I've never lost. I've never won before either.

Japanese: ずいぶん タイドのデカい新人ですな。
English: Quite arrogant for a beginner, aren't you?

Japanese: ‥‥どんな大物だって、 最初は新人さ‥‥
English: Even the mightiest of Redwoods begin their lives as mere saplings.

Japanese: しかも、なんですか！ 法廷内で、仮面なんて‥‥
English: Yes, but... A mask? In a court of law?

Japanese: クッ‥‥！ 知らねえのかい、裁判長。
English: Ha...! Don't you know anything?

Japanese: ヒトはだれでも‥‥ ココロに仮面をつけてるのさ‥‥
English: No matter the man, we all wear masks... Either on our faces or over our hearts.

Japanese: こりゃホンモノだよ、 なるほどくん‥‥
English: This guy is the real deal alright, Nick...

Japanese: （‥‥こんなのばっかりなのか？ 検事って‥‥）
English: (Why does it seem like all prosecutors are the "real deal"...?)

Japanese: やっと会えたな。 ‥‥まるほどう　りゅういち。
English: So we finally meet... Mr. Phoenix Trite!

Japanese: な‥‥なるほどくん！ 知り合いなの？
English: N-Nick! Is he a friend of yours?

Japanese: 知り合いはみんな、ぼくを “なるほどう”って呼ぶよ。
English: No. I don't have any friends that call me "Trite".

Japanese: （あのマスクの下‥‥ いったい、ダレが‥‥？）
English: (Just who is this masked man...?)

Japanese: オレは、アンタと 戦わなけりゃならねえ。
English: I've returned from the depths of Hell...

Japanese: そのために‥‥ 地獄から戻ってきたのさ。
English: To do battle with you.

Japanese: それでは、その。後藤検事。
English: Well then, err, Prosecutor Gobo.

Japanese: “ごとう”じゃねえ。 ゴドー‥‥だぜ。
English: It's not "Gobo". It's Godot... Your Honor.

Japanese: とにかく、 冒頭弁論をおねがいします。
English: In any case, please give your opening statement.

Japanese: そんなモノ、 犬にでも食わせちまいな。
English: Opening statement? Those things are not fit for even dogs to consume.

Japanese: ただ、ひとつだけ。 アンタ。‥‥まるほどう。
English: I have only one thing to say before we start. To you... Mr. Trite.

Japanese: ‥‥なんですか？
English: Wh-What is it?

Japanese: ヒトには“ウツワのデカさ” ってモンがあるぜ。
English: Are you familiar with the saying, "A chain is only as strong as its weakest link"?

Japanese: オレの料理するエモノ‥‥アンタの サラに、盛りつけられるかな‥‥？
English: I wonder... how much you can withstand before you and your case break in two...?

Japanese: それでは、最初の証人の話を 聞くことにしましょう！
English: Hmm... Well then, let's hear from the first witness!

Japanese: えー、自分は‥‥
English: Um, my name is...

Japanese: アンタの名前なんざ、 ダレも聞きたかァねえ。
English: No one has asked for your name, witness.

Japanese: ‥‥むぐ。
English: ...Urk.

Japanese: ダイジなのは、アンタがナニを 知ってるか‥‥それだけだ。
English: The important thing is what you know... That's all.

Japanese: 話してみな。‥‥聞いてやるぜ。
English: Start talking. ...We're listening.

Japanese: は‥‥はあ。
English: Y-Yes, sir.

Japanese: それでは、証人。 まずは‥‥そうですな。
English: Alright, witness. First, let's hear about...

Japanese: ツボを盗んだという怪人について、 説明していただきましょう。
English: What you know about the thief that stole the urn.

Japanese: リョーカイッス！
English: Yes, sir!

Japanese: ～仮面マスクの犯行～
English: -- Mask☆DeMasque's Crimes --

Japanese: 怪人☆仮面マスクは、今から 半年ほど前に現れた、大怪盗ッス。
English: Mask☆DeMasque is a master thief that first started his crime spree 6 months ago.

Japanese: 犯行の前に、かならず予告状を 送ってくる、ナマイキな怪人ッス！
English: He's so confident that he sends his calling card before he even commits the crime!

Japanese: 今度の事件は５回目の犯行で、 やはり高菱屋には予告状が。
English: This was his fifth heist, and as usual, he sent a card on to Lordly Tailor.

Japanese: ヤツのエモノは、最高の値打ちが ある美術品にかぎられているッス。
English: His pattern is to always go after only the most precious art pieces.

Japanese: 以上のコトから、仮面マスクの 犯行と断言できるッス！
English: That's why we're sure it was Mask☆DeMasque, sir. It fits his M. O. to a T!

Japanese: ふむう‥‥怪人☆仮面マスク。 ジツはその正体は‥‥
English: Hmm... So then the actual identity of this Mask☆DeMasque is...

Japanese: ゴドー検事！　あなただった！ なーんて‥‥
English: M-Mr. Godot! Y-You!

Japanese: こここ、コラッ！ 審理中ですぞ、ゴドー検事！
English: W-We're in the middle of a trial here, Mr. Godot!

Japanese: ‥‥闇よりなお深い暗黒をたたえ、 地獄よりも熱く苦い、コーヒー‥‥
English: Blacker than a moonless night, hotter and more bitter than Hell itself... That is coffee.

Japanese: オレは、勝手にやらせてもらうぜ。 ‥‥そっちも、好きに進めてくれ。
English: I'm sure you can grant me at least this much... Your Honor. Oh, please. Proceed.

Japanese: まあ、いいでしょう。
English: Very well. It's only coffee, after all.

Japanese: （‥‥いいのかよ！）
English: (What!? You can't be letting him slide this early in the trial!)

Japanese: では、弁護人。 尋問をおねがいします。
English: Proceed with your cross- examination, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: どうするの？　なるほどくん！
English: Well, Nick? What are you going to do?

Japanese: とにかく‥‥優作くんの正体が ハッキリしない以上‥‥、
English: As long as they haven't brought up Mr. DeLite's identity...

Japanese: “ツボを盗んだのは仮面マスク ではない”と立証するしかないね。
English: ...all we can do is show that it wasn't Mask☆DeMasque who stole the urn.

Japanese: ～仮面マスクの犯行～
English: -- Mask☆DeMasque's Crimes --

Japanese: 怪人☆仮面マスクは、今から 半年ほど前に現れた、大怪盗ッス。
English: Mask☆DeMasque is a master thief that first started his crime spree 6 months ago.

Japanese: 犯行の前に、かならず予告状を 送ってくる、ナマイキな怪人ッス！
English: He's so confident that he sends his calling card before he even commits the crime!

Japanese: 今度の事件は５回目の犯行で、 やはり高菱屋には予告状が。
English: This was his fifth heist, and as usual, he sent a card on to Lordly Tailor.

Japanese: ヤツのエモノは、最高の値打ちが ある美術品にかぎられているッス。
English: His pattern is to always go after only the most precious art pieces.

Japanese: 以上のコトから、仮面マスクの 犯行と断言できるッス！
English: That's why we're sure it was Mask☆DeMasque, sir. It fits his M. O. to a T!

Japanese: なるほどくん！　やっぱり、 ツボを盗んだのは怪人だよ！
English: Nick! It definitely looks like it was Mask☆DeMasque who stole the urn!

Japanese: でも、優作くんが怪人かどうか‥‥ 決定的な証拠はないからね。
English: But there's no real evidence either way as to whether Ron DeLite is Mask☆DeMasque.

Japanese: そんなあ‥‥
English: But... But...

Japanese: それに、彼のまわりから ツボはみつかっていないし。
English: Also, the urn hasn't turned up yet, let alone in connection to Mr. DeLite himself.

Japanese: 仮面マスクがやったに 決まってるのに‥‥
English: So even though we know it was Mask☆DeMasque that did it...

Japanese: （そうじゃないコトを 立証するんだ‥‥とりあえず！）
English: (Maybe for the time being, I should try to show it wasn't Mask☆DeMasque that did it!)

Japanese: あなたは、最初から 捜査に加わっているんですか？
English: Have you been involved in the investigation from the beginning?

Japanese: そッス！　自分は、怪人のコトなら ダレよりも詳しいッス！
English: Yup! Nobody knows more about the thief than me, pal!

Japanese: ‥‥そう！　自分は！　ズヴァリ！ 怪人のエスパーッス！
English: It's true! I'm a... Zvarri...! "Author on thieves"!

Japanese: えすぱー‥‥って、 超能力者じゃなかったっけ。
English: An "author"...? He's written books about thieves?

Japanese: “エキスパート”のつもり かもしれないぞ、もしかしたら。
English: Um, I think he probably meant to say "authority".

Japanese: この自分から逃れるとは、 怪人もなかなか、やるッス！
English: The fact that this guy can slip through even my fingers shows how good he is, pal!

Japanese: （イトノコ刑事だから 逃げられてるんじゃないか？）
English: (It's easy when those fingers happen to be "butter fingers"...)

Japanese: “怪人のコトなら、 ダレよりも詳しい”‥‥？
English: So nobody knows more about the thief than you, huh...?

Japanese: そッス！　怪人本人と、その おかあさんの次に詳しいッス！
English: You got it, pal! Except maybe for the thief's mom, that is!

Japanese: でも‥‥警察を出し抜いている 人物がいませんでしたか？
English: But isn't there someone who knows even more about him than the police?

Japanese: ‥‥もしかして‥‥あの “ズヴァリ！”の探偵ッスか？
English: You don't mean... "Detective Zvarri", do you?

Japanese: ふむ。どなたですかな？ その、“図張探偵”というのは？
English: Hmm... Who is this person? Zvarri? He sounds German...

Japanese: 星威岳　哀牙ッス。‥‥勝手に ヒトの名前を作っちゃダメッス。
English: His name is Luke Atmey, sir. I guess I shouldn't have made up such a silly name for him.

Japanese: （なんだよ‥‥たいして有名じゃ ないぞ、あの探偵‥‥）
English: (What the heck? I guess he's not all that famous after all...)

Japanese: たしかにアイツは、前回の犯行で 怪人のエモノを取り返したッス。
English: Anyway, it's true that he did manage to retrieve the last item the thief stole.

Japanese: ‥‥ほお。 ダメですな、アナタは。
English: ...Oh, I see. It seems you're not the expert you claim to be.

Japanese: むぐ‥‥！　たしかに、自分は今、 怪人に、もてあそばれているッス。
English: ...Urk! I-It looks like the thief is playing with me, even now!

Japanese: （怪人の“エキスパート”といえば あの探偵がいるけど‥‥）
English: (The real "authority" on the thief is the other detective.)

Japanese: （わざわざ聞くほどのコトも ないな‥‥）
English: (I don't need to ask this one any more questions...)

Japanese: あなたは、その予告状を すべて見ているんですか？
English: Have you seen all of these so-called "calling cards"?

Japanese: モチロンッス。 ‥‥ただ。
English: Of course I have. Except...

Japanese: 秘宝展のセキニンシャが 警察に届けなかったせいで‥‥、
English: The person in charge of the Treasure Exhibit never brought their card to the police...

Japanese: 今回の予告状だけは、 犯行後に初めて、見たッス。
English: So I didn't see this one until after the crime occurred.

Japanese: 霧緒さん、アイガ探偵に止められた から、届けなかったんだよね。
English: That's because Detective Atmey stopped Ms. Andrews from taking it to the police.

Japanese: その予告状は、 ホンモノだったのですかな？
English: Was the calling card that Lordly Tailor received authentic?

Japanese: すべての予告状には、 共通のトクチョーがあったッス。
English: Well, all the cards have one common, identifying feature.

Japanese: それらは、一般には 公開されていないッスから‥‥
English: But we're not releasing that information to the general public...

Japanese: ホンモノと考えて まちがいないわけですか‥‥。
English: But you're absolutely certain that this card is real?

Japanese: （怪人のエンブレムの コトだな‥‥）
English: (Gumshoe can't say it out loud but I bet he's talking about Mask☆DeMasque's emblem...)

Japanese: ５回目の犯行で‥‥ ５回目のシッパイですか。
English: His fifth heist... And your fifth screw up, huh?

Japanese: 異議ありッス！
English: Objection, pal!

Japanese: 自分は、およそ約４回ぐらいほど シッパイに近いコトをしてるッス！
English: That ain't fair! Maybe you could say I screwed up four times!

Japanese: しかし！　今回はチガうッス！ 予告を知らなかったッスからねえ！
English: But this last time wasn't my fault! I didn't know about the calling card this time!

Japanese: （イバるなよ‥‥）
English: (You of all people shouldn't be chuckling about this, Detective Gumshoe...)

Japanese: 法廷のみなさんに 言っておきたいッス！
English: I just want everyone here in the courtroom to know something!

Japanese: 怪人の予告状を受け取ったら、 クソ探偵なんかに依頼せず、
English: If you ever get a calling card from this guy, don't call some stupid private eye!

Japanese: 気軽に、警察まで お電話をほしいッス！
English: Call your local police right away. Got me?

Japanese: 哀牙探偵のコト、そうとう 目のカタキにしてるみたいだね。
English: Wow, it looks like he's really got it in for Detective Atmey.

Japanese: 美術品‥‥というと、 たとえば、どんな？
English: Art pieces? Like what, for example?

Japanese: 最初のエモノは、あの有名な 《エマノンの涙》ッス。
English: Well, his first target was the famous "Tear of Emanon".

Japanese: それは、なんですかな？ トクベツしょっぱいナミダか‥‥
English: What's that? Some kind of especially salty teardrop?

Japanese: い、いやいや。青いダイヤッス。 ヤツのエモノで、ただ１つの宝石。
English: N-No, sir. It's a blue diamond. A single, rare diamond.

Japanese: 次のエモノは《ボンゴラの宝冠》 ‥‥ま、アタマに乗せるモノッス。
English: Next was the "Crown of Bongora"... You know, the thing you put on your head.

Japanese: その後、《悪魔の左手》 《メジーナ婦人像》とつづくッス。
English: After that was the "Left Hand of Hades" and then the "Portrait of Mejeena", sir.

Japanese: 最後の《婦人像》は、哀牙探偵の 手によって、美術館に戻ったッス。
English: Detective Atmey retrieved the "Portrait of Mejeena" and returned it to the museum.

Japanese: そして‥‥５回目の犯行が 《倉院のツボ》‥‥ですか。
English: And the target of his fifth and last robbery was the "Sacred Urn"... right?

Japanese: しかし‥‥値打ちのある美術品を 盗んでも、処分に困るのでは‥‥？
English: But isn't it difficult for him to dispose of such famous art pieces...?

Japanese: おそらく怪人は、地下ルートを よく知っていると考えられるッス。
English: Well, we assume he must have some underworld connections.

Japanese: 優作さん‥‥そんなものに 縁がありそうに見えないよね‥‥
English: Somehow, Mr. DeLite doesn't look the type...

Japanese: （“地下”というより“日かげ” っていうイメージだよな‥‥）
English: (Yeah, he's a little too sunny to be hanging out in the underworld...)

Japanese: “以上のコト”と言うのは‥‥？
English: What do you mean when you say, "it fits his M. O. to a T"?

Japanese: あ。私もそれを 聞きたいと思っていました。
English: I was thinking of asking the same thing myself.

Japanese: ううう‥‥ヒトのハナシ、 ちゃんと聞いててほしかったッス。
English: Ugh... I wish you would listen a little more closely, pal.

Japanese: まずは、予告状ッス。コイツは、 まちがいなくホンモノだったッス。
English: First of all, there's the calling card. We're 100% certain it's authentic.

Japanese: そして、こちらの警備システムを 読んだかのような手口。
English: Then there's the fact that he seemed to know all about the security system.

Japanese: さらに、高価な美術品のみを狙う。 ‥‥以上、ヤツの犯行の特徴ッス。
English: And finally, his target was an art piece. These are all part of the thief's modus operandi.

Japanese: 今回の犯行も、それらの条件が 当てはまる‥‥と？
English: And so, since this robbery seems to fit all those conditions...?

Japanese: そッス！　したがって、この事件も 仮面マスクのシワザッス！
English: That's right! It means that Mask☆DeMasque is behind it!

Japanese: ‥‥ちょっといいですか、 イトノコ刑事。
English: ...Can I ask you a little something, Detective Gumshoe?

Japanese: アンタがそう言って“ちょっと”で すんだコトは一度もないッス。
English: ...Just hearing the "little" in that question is making me nervous.

Japanese: 『怪人のエモノは、最高の値打ち モノだけ』‥‥という話でしたね。
English: You said that he always "goes after the most precious art pieces", right?

Japanese: そッス。
English: That's right, pal.

Japanese: しかし‥‥今回だけは、 そうではなかった。
English: But there's one problem... That's not what he did in this case.

Japanese: 倉院のツボは、“秘宝”というほど たいしたシロモノではない！
English: The supposedly "priceless" urn doesn't exactly rise to the level of "precious art"!

Japanese: なな、なんスと‥‥！
English: Wh-What do you mean!?

Japanese: なるほどくん！　そ、それは、 あたしに対するチョーセンなの！
English: N-Nick! How can you say such a terrible thing!?

Japanese: いやいや。‥‥金銭的には、だよ。 悪いけど、アレは売れないだろ？
English: No! I meant from a financial point of view! I mean, it wouldn't fetch a good price.

Japanese: ‥‥ゴドー検事、どうなのですか？ あのツボの価値は‥‥
English: Well, Prosecutor Godot? What is the value of that urn?

Japanese: 美術鑑定家たちのハナシでは、 ツボに値段はつけられないそうだ。
English: The appraisers I spoke to said they couldn't attach a price to it.

Japanese: ‥‥もちろん、悪いイミで。
English: And I mean that in the worst sense.

Japanese: ‥‥つまり！
English: So in other words...

Japanese: 倉院のツボは、仮面マスクが狙う エモノとして、不自然なのです！
English: It was not the kind of item that Mask☆DeMasque would normally go after!

Japanese: うぐむッ！
English: Urk!

Japanese: ふむう‥‥つまり弁護側の 主張は、こういうコトですかな？
English: Hmm... If I understand you correctly, Mr. Wright...

Japanese: 今回の事件‥‥倉院のツボの盗難は 仮面マスクのシワザではない‥‥！
English: ...you're saying that the theft of the Sacred Urn was not the work of Mask☆DeMasque...!

Japanese: そ、そういうコト‥‥です。
English: Y-Yes... That's what I'm saying.

Japanese: （ムジュンを指摘したら、自然に そうなっちゃったんだけど‥‥）
English: (Actually all I did was point out the contradiction. The argument made itself, but...)

Japanese: たしかに‥‥まず、そこを ハッキリさせるべきでしょう。
English: Well... First of all, we need to get this issue cleared up.

Japanese: 今回の犯行が、仮面マスクの シワザか、そうでないか‥‥？
English: Was this last robbery the work of Mask☆DeMasque or not?

Japanese: いかがですかッ、ゴドー検事！
English: What do you have to say about this, Mr. Godot?

Japanese: ‥‥このコーヒー‥‥ ゴドー・ブレンド１０７号。
English: This coffee here... It's my own special blend. I call it Godot #107.

Japanese: 刺すような酸味を取るべきか、 からみつく苦味を取るべきか‥‥
English: I'm trying to decide whether to cut down on the acidity or the bitterness...

Japanese: 今、オレに関心があるのは、 それだけだぜ。‥‥まるほどう！
English: That's the only thing I've got on my mind right now... ...Mr. Trite!

Japanese: な、なんですか。
English: Wh-What!?

Japanese: ‥‥アンタもオトコなら、 自分のケツは自分でふくコトだ。
English: If you're really a man, you should clean up your own mess.

Japanese: あの‥‥。悪いけど イミがわかりません。
English: Umm... Sorry, but I don't get what you mean.

Japanese: ツボを盗んだのが、もし 怪人の犯行でないとしたら‥‥
English: If you're saying it wasn't Mask☆DeMasque that stole the urn...

Japanese: その手口をマネた、仮面マスクの “ニセモノ”ってコトになる。
English: ...then it must be someone imitating Mask☆DeMasque's methods... A fake.

Japanese: に‥‥ニセ☆仮面マスク！
English: A-A Fake☆DeMasque?

Japanese: ものスゴくウソっぽいヒビキだね。 ‥‥あたしは好きだけど。
English: Fake☆DeMasque? That sounds so ridiculous. ...But I like it.

Japanese: ‥‥オレがこのコーヒーの問題に 答えを出す前に、立証してくれよ。
English: Now, before I decide on my coffee, I believe some proof is in order, Mr. Trite.

Japanese: 犯行当夜、高菱屋に現れた怪人が ニセモノだった、ってな‥‥
English: Proof that the person who appeared at Lordly Tailor that night was actually a fake.

Japanese: 検事のタイドにはちょっとハラが 立ちますが、主張はもっともです。
English: Hmm... Though I don't approve of Mr. Godot's behavior, his point is valid.

Japanese: 弁護人。‥‥よろしいですな！
English: Mr. Wright. ...We're waiting.

Japanese: （どうやら‥‥なんとしても 提示しなくちゃならないようだ）
English: (It looks like I'm going to have to prove it...)

Japanese: （あの晩、高菱屋に現れたのが ニセ☆仮面マスクだった証拠！）
English: (I need proof that the person at Lordly Tailor that night was in fact, Fake☆DeMasque!)

Japanese: ‥‥この証拠品が、いったい ナニを立証しているのですかな？
English: What is this evidence supposed to prove?

Japanese: クッ‥‥！　どうやら、リッパに 立証しているようだぜ。
English: Ha...! Well, it proves someone here is a fake alright.

Japanese: ‥‥そこの弁護士が ニセモノだ、ってな‥‥。
English: ...It proves that this lawyer is a fake.

Japanese: （検事って、みんな ウマいコト言うよな‥‥）
English: (These prosecutors sure know how to twist the knife...)

Japanese: 感心してる場合じゃないでしょ！ 考えなおそうよ！
English: This is no time to be impressed! Think it over again!

Japanese: ツボを盗んだとき‥‥ 怪人、写真を撮られているよね。
English: When he stole the urn, his photo was taken, right?

Japanese: あの怪人に、ニセモノっぽいところ でもあれば、いいんだけどねえ‥‥
English: Who knows? Maybe there's something on there that shows he's a fake...

Japanese: （‥‥哀牙探偵から もらった写真、か‥‥）
English: (Hmm... Is she talking about that photo we got from Atmey?)

Japanese: ‥‥そろそろ よろしいですかな、弁護人。
English: Are you ready yet, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: その証拠は、これです。
English: The proof is right here.

Japanese: これは‥‥防犯カメラが 撮影した写真‥‥ですか。
English: This looks like... a photo taken by a security camera.

Japanese: この写真には‥‥１カ所、 おかしな部分があります。
English: But if you look closely... you'll notice something peculiar about it.

Japanese: ほお‥‥それなら、ついでだ。 チョイと教えてくれるかい。
English: Ha...! Well then, why don't you go ahead and show us what it is.

Japanese: その人差し指で‥‥ “ヘンな部分”とやら、を。
English: Go on. Use this pointer and show us just what about this picture is so "peculiar".

Japanese: それは、ここです！ ‥‥おそらく。
English: It's right here. ...I think.

Japanese: ‥‥そこが“ヘンな部分” なのですか？
English: Is that what was so "peculiar"?

Japanese: クッ‥‥！　その人差し指‥‥ 示すトコロがちがうようだぜ。
English: Ha...! The place that you're pointing to is all wrong.

Japanese: な‥‥なんだと！
English: Wh... What do you mean!

Japanese: “ヘンな部分”‥‥それは、 アンタ自身のアタマ、ってことサ。
English: The only thing "peculiar" in this courtroom... is on top of your head.

Japanese: なるほどくん！ ヘアスタイルをヒハンされてるよ！
English: Nick! Are you going to let him get away with making fun of your hair!?

Japanese: （‥‥おそらく、 そういうコトじゃない）
English: (Yes... At least until I know he can't use his goggles to fry me or something...)

Japanese: と、とにかく！　この写真には ヘンな部分があるハズです！
English: W-Well anyway! There is a strange spot in this photo!

Japanese: ほお‥‥まだ、ヤル気かい。 それなら、教えてくれよ‥‥
English: Ha...! Still haven't given up? So what is it then?

Japanese: それは、ここです！ ‥‥モチロン。
English: It's right here, of course!

Japanese: 仮面マスク‥‥ですか？
English: You mean... Mask☆DeMasque?

Japanese: さて。ここに、仮面マスクに関する 資料があります。
English: I have here a piece of reference I would like the court to take a look at.

Japanese: 今朝、あたしが買った ブロマイドじゃない‥‥
English: Isn't that the publicity photo I bought this morning...?

Japanese: 問題は‥‥仮面マスクのムネに ついている、エンブレムです。
English: The problem I have with the security camera photo is the broach on DeMasque's chest.

Japanese: えんぶれむ‥‥？ なんですかな、それは。
English: A Breach? Here!? Bailiff! Get my steed! We need to retreat at once!

Japanese: ‥‥カタいアップリケ みたいなモノです。
English: ...A broach, Your Honor. It's a sort of clasp for holding one's cape on.

Japanese: あっぷりけ‥‥ああ！　あの。
English: A clasp, eh... Ah! I see now!

Japanese: たしかに、現場写真のほうの 仮面マスクは‥‥
English: But the Mask☆DeMasque in the security camera photo...

Japanese: エンブレムがないッス！
English: Ahh!! He has no broach!!

Japanese: 予告状を見ればわかるとおり、 これは仮面マスクのシンボルです！
English: That broach is the same as the emblem on DeMasque's calling card and serves as his symbol.

Japanese: しかし‥‥高菱屋に現れた怪人は それをつけていなかった‥‥
English: But the thief that broke into Lordly Tailor wasn't wearing a broach...

Japanese: ‥‥つまり！　この仮面マスクは、 ニセモノなのです！
English: In other words, this "Mask☆DeMasque" is a fake!

Japanese: ま‥‥ またやられたッスウウゥゥゥゥッ！
English: I-I've been fooled agaaaaaain!

Japanese: せ、静粛に！　たしかに‥‥ 反論しようがありません。
English: O-Order! It's true... Undeniably true...

Japanese: イトノコギリ刑事！ あなた‥‥また、こんなミスをッ！
English: Detective Gumshoe! How... How could you have overlooked this!?

Japanese: す‥‥すまねッス！ 自分はまた、こんな‥‥
English: I-I'm sorry, sir! I don't know how I...

Japanese: おいおい‥‥。
English: Hey now...

Japanese: ‥‥パーティーなら、 オレもまぜてくれよ。
English: ...If you're gonna have a pity party, invite me, too!

Japanese: ご‥‥ゴドー検事！ あなたもあなたです！
English: M-Mr. Godot! You deserve some blame in this, too!

Japanese: こんな大きな アップリケを見逃すなんて‥‥
English: How could you have overlooked such a large broach...?

Japanese: クッ‥‥！　アンタたちが お探しのシロモノ‥‥
English: Ha...! The broach you're talking about...

Japanese: ひょっとして、コイツのことかい？
English: Do you mean this?

Japanese: あッ‥‥！
それは‥‥エンブレム！
English: Th-That's...! Mask☆DeMasque's broach!

Japanese: い、いったい‥‥どこでそれを！
English: Wh-Where did you find it!?

Japanese: 証拠はさびしがり屋さ。いつだって 現場にかくれてやがる。
English: Well I've always had a good nose for evidence... I got it at the crime scene.

Japanese: このレディなら、でっかい仏像の カゲに落ちていたぜ。
English: It was hidden in the shadow of a big female Buddha statue.

Japanese: （“仏像”‥‥ 綾里　供子の像のコトだな）
English: ("Buddha statue"...? He must mean the Ami Fey statue.)

Japanese: な‥‥なんで自分にも 教えてくれなかったッスか！
English: Wh-Why didn't you tell me about that, sir!?

Japanese: ‥‥証拠品は、自分のポケットに しまうことにしてるのさ。
English: ...I always put evidence away in my pocket.

Japanese: ‥‥ここがイチバン 安全だからな‥‥
English: ...After all, it's the safest place for crucial evidence.

Japanese: くううう‥‥ シビれるねえ、なるほどくん。
English: Grrr... This guy is one cool customer.

Japanese: シビれるのは早いぜ ‥‥おじょうちゃん。
English: It's a little early to be shaken up, isn't it, little lady?

Japanese: コイツに、残っていやがったのさ。 ‥‥熱いキスマークが。
English: ...That friend of yours left pretty little hickeys on there, too.

Japanese: き‥‥きすまあく？
English: H-Hickeys?

Japanese: ‥‥ヤケるじゃねえか。 被告人‥‥天杉　優作の指紋さ。
English: Figuratively speaking, of course... I'm referring to Ron DeLite's fingerprints.
Whaaa...?

Japanese: なんですって！　被告人の指紋が ‥‥エンブレムにッ！
English: What!? The defendant's fingerprints are on the broach!?

Japanese: 静粛に！　静粛に！
English: Order! Order in the court!

Japanese: ゴドー検事！ エンブレムを提出してください！
English: Mr. Godot! Let's see that broach!

Japanese: オレのコイビトだ。 ‥‥ダイジにあつかってくれよ。
English: I've grown attached to my metallic girlfriend here. ...Take good care of her.

Japanese: ふむう‥‥どうも、衣装から 引きちぎられたようですね。
English: Hmm... She-- I mean, it appears to have been torn off some clothing.

Japanese: エンブレムのウラに、 布の切れはしが残っています。
English: There's a little bit of cloth left on the back.

Japanese: どうやら‥‥あの晩、現場で タフなファイトがあったようだぜ。
English: Obviously there must have been a big struggle that night at the crime scene.

Japanese: （さ‥‥サイアクの展開だ‥‥）
English: (Uh oh... Phoenix, we have a problem...)

Japanese: 証拠品《怪人のエンブレム》の データを法廷記録にファイルした。
English: DeMasque's Broach added to the Court Record.

Japanese: クッ‥‥！　このゴドー、 ウカツにナメちゃダメさ。
English: Ha...! You mess with Godot...

Japanese: ‥‥ヤケドしちゃうぜ！
English: ...and you get burned.

Japanese: （どうやら‥‥ここまでの流れは アイツの計算どおり、か‥‥）
English: (Grr... He's been playing me like a violin...)

Japanese: さて、裁判長。 ‥‥そろそろ、次の証人を呼ぶぜ。
English: Well Judge, I'm about ready to call my next witness.

Japanese: え。自分はもう、おしまいッスか。 まだ、ナニも立証してないのに。
English: Huh? You're done with me? But I haven't proved anything yet.

Japanese: アンタ自身の無能ぶりを立証した。 ‥‥それでヨシとしようぜ。
English: You've proven your own incompetence. That's good enough.

Japanese: ちっともよくないッス！
English: ...That doesn't sound good at all.

Japanese: それでは、係官！ 次の証人を入廷させなさい！
English: Bailiff! Bring the next witness into the courtroom!

Japanese: （いよいよ‥‥ 名探偵さんの登場、か‥‥）
English: (Finally time for the Ace Detective to make his appearance, huh...?)

Japanese: １秒の時間は、コーヒーの１滴。 さっさと、おっ始めるとするぜ‥‥
English: One second is one drip of the coffee pot. Let's hurry it up.

Japanese: ‥‥シッ！　ダマって！
English: SHHH! SILENCE!

Japanese: クックックックックッ‥‥ どうやら、見えたようですな。
English: Hee hee hee hee hee... Now I see. It's all becoming clear.

Japanese: なな、なんですか？
English: Wh-What's clear?

Japanese: ‥‥ズヴァリ！ 美しきスイリが我にささやく真実。
English: ...Zvarri! The truth has once again been elegantly revealed to me!

Japanese: 貴方がたは、裁判長と‥‥おそらく 検事。‥‥それもコーヒー好きの！
English: What we have here is... A judge and a prosecutor. ...A coffee maniac, at that!

Japanese: ‥‥ちがいますかな？
English: ...Am I correct?

Japanese: そ‥‥そりゃま、そうですが。
English: W-Well, yes. That's right.

Japanese: クッ‥‥！　アンタ、 なかなかやるじゃねえか。
English: Ha...! Not bad. Not bad at all.

Japanese: このゴドーの正体を見抜いたの‥‥ アンタが初めてだぜ。
English: You're the first person that's ever been able to penetrate my secret veil.

Japanese: やあれ、検事殿。 自己紹介がおくれたようです。
English: Well, Sir Prosecutor. Let me introduce myself.

Japanese: 我が名は星威岳　哀牙。 名探偵という名の、孤高の一番星。
English: My name is Luke Atmey, Ace Detective and rising star illuminating the heavens.

Japanese: キョーレツなのが そろっちゃったね‥‥
English: Boy, these two make a perfect pair...

Japanese: しかも‥‥ちょっと 気が合いそうだぞ、あのふたり。
English: They'd either be best friends or they'd tear each other's heads off.

Japanese: あなたは事件当夜、たったヒトリで 警備をしていたそうですね。
English: I've heard that on the night of the crime, you were all alone on security detail.

Japanese: ‥‥さよう。
English: ...You have heard correctly.

Japanese: わがマナコは、イトノコギリ刑事 １００人にまさる特別製なのです。
English: My specially-made monocle is worth more than a hundred Detective Gumshoes.

Japanese: ‥‥まあ。あの刑事のマナコが 見えていれば、のハナシですが。
English: ...If Detective Gumshoe was worth anything, that is.

Japanese: ふむう‥‥
English: Hmm...

Japanese: （あの探偵がなぜ、たったヒトリで 警備にあたっていたか‥‥？）
English: (Why was this guy all by himself anyway...?)

Japanese: （何か、理由があるはずだ。 ‥‥かならず！）
English: (There must be some reason. I'm sure of it!)

Japanese: では、その特別製のマナコで 目撃したことを証言してください！
English: Well then, tell us what this special monocle of yours witnessed!

Japanese: ～目撃したこと～
English: -- What I Witnessed --

Japanese: あの晩‥‥日付が変わって、 １時間ほどのこと‥‥。
English: It was approximately 1 o'clock in the morning... just after the date changed.

Japanese: きゃつめ‥‥宿敵たるライヴァル・ 仮面マスクが舞いおりたッ！
English: That's when my nemesis, the infamous Mask☆DeMasque, dancingly descended upon me!

Japanese: ふり返ろうとした、そのせつな！ ホコリ高きアタマに、一撃がッ！
English: Just as I began to turn, the coward struck a fierce blow upon my noble head!

Japanese: 不覚！　怪人と戦うことすらできず ‥‥我が意識は、ツユと消えたッ！
English: Darkness swallowed me before I could land a single strike. When I awoke, he was gone.

Japanese: ３０分後‥‥非常電話にて 警察に通報を入れたのです！
English: Thirty minutes later, I used an emergency phone to notify the police!

Japanese: ‥‥犯人の姿そのものは ハッキリ見ていないのですか。
English: So you didn't get a clear look at the criminal?

Japanese: 特別製のマナコは、ナニモノも 見落としたりはしませんぞ。
English: My specially-made monocle never misses a thing.

Japanese: ただし！　目に入らなかったモノは このかぎりにあらず。
English: However! That is limited to things that fall within my own visual range!

Japanese: けだし、トーゼンと言えましょう。
English: But of course, that's only natural.

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥なぜ証人がそこまでムネを 張れるのか、理解に苦しみます。
English: ...I fail to see why the witness seems so proud of his performance that evening.

Japanese: まあ、ご老人。‥‥これだけは 言っておきましょう。
English: Well, Sir Old-timer, let me explain. We are not speaking of any ordinary thief...

Japanese: ヤツこそは、我が好敵手にして ライヴァルたる仮面マスクだった！
English: ...but of the king of thieves! The great Mask☆DeMasque, my arch-enemy!

Japanese: 我がケイケンとカン、その他 モロモロがそう告げております。
English: That is what my instincts and my years of experience tell me!

Japanese: ‥‥ふむう‥‥。 では、弁護人。とにかく尋問を。
English: Hmm... Very well. Proceed with the cross-examination, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: ～目撃したこと～
English: -- What I Witnessed --

Japanese: あの晩‥‥日付が変わって、 １時間ほどのこと‥‥。
English: It was approximately 1 o'clock in the morning... just after the date changed.

Japanese: きゃつめ‥‥宿敵たるライヴァル・ 仮面マスクが舞いおりたッ！
English: That's when my nemesis, the infamous Mask☆DeMasque, dancingly descended upon me!

Japanese: ふり返ろうとした、そのせつな！ ホコリ高きアタマに、一撃がッ！
English: Just as I began to turn, the coward struck a fierce blow upon my noble head!

Japanese: 不覚！　怪人と戦うことすらできず ‥‥我が意識は、ツユと消えたッ！
English: Darkness swallowed me before I could land a single strike. When I awoke, he was gone.

Japanese: ３０分後‥‥非常電話にて 警察に通報を入れたのです！
English: Thirty minutes later, I used an emergency phone to notify the police!

Japanese: なんか、アヤしいよねー。 ‥‥アイガ探偵。
English: There's something suspicious about Detective Atmey...

Japanese: 犯人が入ってきたのに 気がつかないなんて。
English: How could he not have noticed when the thief came in?

Japanese: “戦う前に、キゼツした‥‥” 哀牙探偵はそう言ってるけど‥‥、
English: Also, he says he was knocked unconscious before he could fight back...

Japanese: ‥‥そんなはずはないんだ。 あの証拠品とムジュンしている。
English: But that can't be right. It contradicts the evidence.

Japanese: “あの証拠品”‥‥？
English: Huh? Which piece...?

Japanese: （モンダイは‥‥どうして、そんな あからさまなウソをつくのか？）
English: (The real question is... Why would he tell such an obvious lie?)

Japanese: ということは‥‥１２日の 午前１時ごろ、になりますね？
English: So that would be one o'clock on the morning of the 12th, correct?

Japanese: なかなかミゴトな スイリですな、弁護士殿。
English: That's an impressive deduction, Sir Lawyer.

Japanese: あなたは、現場で見張っていた。 ‥‥どこにいたんですか？
English: You were on security duty that night. Where exactly were you at the time?

Japanese: さよう‥‥。地下倉庫の コムピュウタのあたり、ですな。
English: A penetrating question! I was in the basement warehouse, near the computer.

Japanese: （コンピュータ‥‥パソコンの コトか‥‥）
English: (Near the computer, huh...)

Japanese: 身をかくしていたワケでは ないようですね。
English: So then you weren't trying to remain hidden, I take it?

Japanese: 今までの事件では、我はつねに、 身をかくして怪人を待っていた‥‥
English: Up to this point, I have tried to remain concealed while waiting for the thief...

Japanese: （たしか、きのうも そんなコトを言ってたな）
English: (Yeah, he said the same thing yesterday, too.)

Japanese: イトノコさんも『現場でヤツを 見たことない』って言ってたね。
English: Gumshoe also said that he never saw him at the crime scene...

Japanese: だからこそ！　ゆうべは あえて、かくれなかったのです！
English: Precisely! That is precisely why I chose NOT to hide last night.

Japanese: あえて我が身をさらすことで、 プレッシャアを与える作戦ですな。
English: I knew that by NOT concealing myself, I would be putting pressure on the thief.

Japanese: （アッサリ殴られたクセに‥‥）
English: (Looks like the thief was the one applying pressure... On your pidgeony head, that is.)

Japanese: とにかく、その時刻‥‥！
English: In any case!

Japanese: 舞いおりた‥‥って、 どこからですか？
English: "Dancingly descended"...? From where exactly?

Japanese: ま。それは、入り口のドアから でしょうな、おそらく。
English: Well... From the entrance, I suppose. Where else?

Japanese: （“舞い”も“おりる”も カンケイないじゃないか！）
English: (So in actuality, he neither "danced" nor "descended"! ...Someone please save me.)

Japanese: あの‥‥。どうして怪人に 気がつかなかったんですか？
English: Um... So how is it that you didn't notice the thief?

Japanese: 我がマナコは怪人のカゲを求め、 我が耳は怪人の足音を探していた。
English: My eyes were looking for the thief's shadow while my ears listened for his footfalls.

Japanese: ‥‥だのに！　ヤツはその上を 行ったのです！　おそらく‥‥
English: But even so, the dastardly criminal managed to sneak up on me...

Japanese: 怪人め、地味なマントを着て いいクツをはいていたのでしょう。
English: It can only be due to his subtly camouflaged cape and soft-soled shoes.

Japanese: （アンタ、ホントに 名探偵なのかよ‥‥）
English: I hereby dub you "Ace Dunce".)

Japanese: そのとき、犯人のカオは 見なかったんですか？
English: You didn't see the criminal's face when that happened?

Japanese: ‥‥それこそは、 “微”にして“妙”ですな。
English: ...Well, that's the difficult part. How should I put it...

Japanese: 怪人の仮面のマスクは見た ‥‥そうキオクしております。
English: I saw his mask... That's all that I can recall.

Japanese: ふむう‥‥ややアイマイですな。
English: Hmm... That's not very solid as far as testimony goes.

Japanese: しかし‥‥このとおり、我が 第３のマナコたる防犯キャメラが、
English: However... Fortunately I had my third monocle, the security camera, at the ready.

Japanese: その姿をシカととらえている。 ‥‥じゅうぶんではないですかな？
English: It captured his image perfectly... This should be sufficient, I believe?

Japanese: たしかに‥‥写真がホンモノで あれば、モンダイないでしょうな。
English: Hmm, well, as long as this photo is authentic, I don't see a problem here.

Japanese: いかがですか？　ゴドー検事。
English: Well, Mr. Godot? Do you have a problem with the photo?

Japanese: ‥‥それでは、 証言をつづけてください。
English: Good. Then let's continue with the testimony.

Japanese: 襲われて、キゼツ‥‥ 何もできなかったんですか？
English: Attacked and knocked unconscious? And you weren't able to do a thing?

Japanese: ‥‥名探偵のくせに。
English: That's certainly some very impressive detective work.

Japanese: あいや、弁護士殿！ 貴方‥‥いきなり殴られたことは？
English: Hmph! Well, Sir Lawyer! Have you ever been suddenly struck on the head?

Japanese: え！　あ、ありますけど。 ‥‥消火器で。
English: Huh!? W-Well... Actually, yes. By a fire extinguisher.

Japanese: その結果、どうなりましたかな？
English: And what happened?

Japanese: たしか‥‥そのままキゼツして‥‥
English: ...I-I was knocked out.

Japanese: キオクもなくしちゃったんだよね！
English: And you lost your memory, too!

Japanese: ‥‥さよう。貴方に我を せめる資格はありませぬな。
English: You see? You have no right to look down on me then, do you?

Japanese: 記憶を保っているぶん、我がアタマ のほうが優秀とも言えましょう。
English: The only reason I didn't lose my memory was because I have more brains to begin with.

Japanese: （コイツのアタマ‥‥“反省する” って回路はないのかよ‥‥）
English: (He may have brains, but the wiring to the self-reflection part seems to be severed...)

Japanese: ‥‥さて。このようにして、 我が意識はとぎれた。そして‥‥
English: In any case, that was how I was knocked senseless. And then...

Japanese: その“３０分”というのは‥‥？
English: About this "30 minutes"...

Japanese: 我が意識が黄泉の世界へと旅立ち、 黄昏の彼岸を彷徨いし、玉響‥‥。
English: My silver cord was loosened and my soul fled to the Golden Halls of Elysium...

Japanese: ‥‥あいかわらず読めないし、 イミもゼンゼンわかんないよ。
English: As usual, I have no idea what this guy is saying.

Japanese: 《キゼツしてノビていた》 ‥‥たぶん、そういうコトだな。
English: I think he's saying that he was out cold.

Japanese: では‥‥ その３０分間のコトは‥‥？
English: So... What happened during these 30 minutes?

Japanese: ダレにも知ることのできない‥‥ 空白の時間、というワケですな！
English: No one can say, Your Honor... That span of time has truly vanished into the ether!

Japanese: （おまえが言うなよ！）
English: (Just what is he going on about!?)

Japanese: ‥‥哀牙さん。
English: ...Mr. Atmey.

Japanese: その特別製のマナコで、 これを見てもらえますか。
English: Could you take a look at this with that special monocle of yours?

Japanese: これはしたり！　我が好敵手にして ライヴァルなる怪人なれば大盗人！
English: Aha! This belongs to the criminal mastermind, my arch-nemesis, Mask☆DeMasque!

Japanese: かの、仮面マスクめのエムブレム ‥‥ですな。
English: It is, in point of fact, Mask☆DeMasque's broach!

Japanese: 現場の地下倉庫に落ちていました。 ‥‥なぜでしょうね？
English: It was found on the floor of the basement warehouse. I wonder how that happened?

Japanese: フッ‥‥カンタンなコト。 ノリづけがアマかったのでしょう。
English: Ha... Elementary, my dear lawyer. Obviously it wasn't glued on well enough.

Japanese: ‥‥コイツには、衣装から 引きちぎられたアトがあります。
English: Not quite. It clearly shows signs of having been ripped off a piece of clothing.

Japanese: 引きちぎられた‥‥あっ！
English: Ripped off...? Aha!

Japanese: ‥‥事件当夜、“怪人”は 何者かと格闘したものと思われる！
English: ...We can only deduce that the thief struggled with someone that night!

Japanese: エンブレムがムシり取られたのは、 そのときしか考えられません。
English: That's the only thing I can think of.

Japanese: そして、犯人と格闘できたのは‥‥
English: And there's only one person that was in a position to have a struggle with the thief.

Japanese: たった１人で現場を見張っていた あなたしか、いないのです！
English: The only person that was on security duty that night... You, Detective Atmey!

Japanese: ‥‥グッ！
English: ...Urk!

Japanese: 哀牙探偵！　あなたは怪人と 格闘をしているハズです！
English: Detective Atmey! You must have fought with the thief that night!

Japanese: なのに、なぜ‥‥ ウソの証言をするのですか？
English: So, why did you lie in your testimony to the court?

Japanese: ‥‥証人！　偽証は、 リッパな犯罪ですぞ！
English: Witness! Giving false testimony is a serious crime!

Japanese: あ、あいや！ ‥‥あいや、待たれよ、ご老人。
English: Err, I... N-No! W-Wait just a moment, Sir Old-timer!

Japanese: 私をごインキョみたいに 呼ばないように！
English: Don't talk to me like I'm living in a nursing home!

Japanese: クックックックックッ‥‥ 思い出しましたぞ、裁判長殿。
English: Hee hee hee he he he... I just remembered, Your Honor.

Japanese: 最近、多くの事件を抱えているので ‥‥コンランしたようです。
English: I was just confused because I've been dealing with so many cases lately.

Japanese: デキるオトコは、サバける仕事の 数で決まる‥‥オレの哲学だぜ。
English: The true measure of a man is in the amount of work he does. That's what I always say.

Japanese: なるほどくん、 いつも１コだよね。シゴト。
English: Nick, you can only handle one case at a time... Isn't that right?

Japanese: うるさいな。
English: You talk too much.

Japanese: 証人！　つまり、あなたは‥‥ 犯人と格闘したと言うのですか？
English: Witness! So are you now saying that you and the thief fought?

Japanese: おっと。おしゃべりは そこまでだぜ‥‥裁判長。
English: Hold on. That's quite enough... Your Honor.

Japanese: ‥‥お、おしゃべり‥‥
English: E-Excuse me...?

Japanese: ダイジなコトは、証言で聞く。 そいつが‥‥オレのルールだぜ。
English: Save the big questions for the testimony. That's one of my rules.

Japanese: あいや！　了解しましたぞ。 この哀牙、燃ゆるイノチにかけて。
English: Indeed! I understand! I, Luke Atmey, agree completely!

Japanese: ～犯人との格闘～
English: -- Fight With The Thief --

Japanese: さよう。たしかに、この哀牙が 扉から目をはなしたのは、ジジツ。
English: Indeed, it's true that I looked away from the door for a brief moment.

Japanese: しかし‥‥、そんなコトで この名探偵をたばかるのは不可能！
English: However... Luke Atmey cannot be so easily discombobulated!

Japanese: 怪人は、かたわらの武器を手に取り ‥‥我らは、イノチのやりとりを！
English: Unfortunately, the thief grabbed a weapon from the side and... rendered me senseless.

Japanese: ゼントルメンの武器は、コブシのみ ‥‥それが、アダとなりました。
English: A true gentleman fights only with his own fists... But they were not enough.

Japanese: ヤツめの一撃がコツンと！ ‥‥それで、すべてですな。
English: His first blow struck true! Bam! ...And that's all she wrote.

Japanese: やはり、仮面マスクを 目撃していたワケですか‥‥？
English: So in the end, you did catch a glimpse of Mask☆DeMasque?

Japanese: さよう。
English: Correct.

Japanese: ふり返りザマにやられたのは、 ヤツの３回目の犯行のときでした。
English: It was during his third crime that he struck me from behind.

Japanese: どうやら、記憶が少々 コンランしてしまったようです。
English: It seems that my memory has become a tad jumbled, so to speak.

Japanese: ふむう‥‥まあたしかに、 よくあるコトですな。
English: Hmmm... Well, that's certainly understandable.

Japanese: 私も、証言がどの事件のものか、 よくわからなくなりますからな。
English: I, myself, always get confused about which testimony goes with which case.

Japanese: （‥‥そりゃマズいだろ‥‥）
English: (Th-That can't be good...)

Japanese: ～犯人との格闘～
English: -- Fight With The Thief --

Japanese: さよう。たしかに、この哀牙が 扉から目をはなしたのは、ジジツ。
English: Indeed, it's true that I looked away from the door for a brief moment.

Japanese: しかし‥‥、そんなコトで この名探偵をたばかるのは不可能！
English: However... Luke Atmey cannot be so easily discombobulated!

Japanese: 怪人は、かたわらの武器を手に取り ‥‥我らは、イノチのやりとりを！
English: Unfortunately, the thief grabbed a weapon from the side and... rendered me senseless.

Japanese: ゼントルメンの武器は、コブシのみ ‥‥それが、アダとなりました。
English: A true gentleman fights only with his own fists... But they were not enough.

Japanese: ヤツめの一撃がコツンと！ ‥‥それで、すべてですな。
English: His first blow struck true! Bam! ...And that's all she wrote.

Japanese: どうかな？　なるほどくん。
English: Well what do you think, Nick?

Japanese: ‥‥１つだけ、 ハッキリしているコトがあるよ。
English: Well, there's one thing that I'm absolutely sure of now.

Japanese: え！　なに、なに？
English: Yeah? What is it!?

Japanese: アイツは‥‥哀牙探偵は、 まだ、何かをかくそうとしている！
English: This Luke Atmey guy... He's definitely hiding something!

Japanese: そ、それって‥‥ どういうコトかな？
English: B-But why?

Japanese: なんとなく‥‥この事件の真相が 見えてきたような気がするよ。
English: I think I'm starting to figure out what really happened that night.

Japanese: （‥‥あの探偵の“正体”が‥‥）
English: (...And about the true nature of this "detective".)

Japanese: 背にカベをつけて応戦をしましたが ‥‥我がマユアイに怪人の一撃が！
English: I put my back to the wall to fight, but the thief's blow landed upon my third eye.

Japanese: そもそも、どうして 扉から目をはなしたんですか？
English: So why did you look away from the door anyway?

Japanese: 地下倉庫には、防犯キャメラ以外に 多くのセンサアがしかけてあった。
English: In addition to the camera, I had prepared a variety of other sensors as well.

Japanese: その１つが、ピピと反応した！ すわ、デエタをカクニンせねばッ！
English: The alarm on one of those had gone off, so I had to check the data.

Japanese: ‥‥我は、コムピュウタのところへ 行ったのです。‥‥そう、華麗に！
English: That's why I went to the computer! ...Elegantly, of course!

Japanese: パソコンをいじっていて、 スキができた、ってワケですか‥‥
English: So you were momentarily vulnerable when you were working on the computer?

Japanese: （どうする？　もう少し、 聞いてみるか‥‥？）
English: (What should I do? Should I ask some more questions...?)

Japanese: （とにかく、哀牙探偵が扉から 目をはなしたのはジジツらしい）
English: (Well, we know for sure that Atmey looked away from the door.)

Japanese: （もっと、他のコトを聞いたほうが よさそうだな‥‥）
English: (I think I should ask about something else now...)

Japanese: わかりました。あなたは、扉から 目をはなした。‥‥そして？
English: Alright. So you looked away from the door. ...And then?

Japanese: その‥‥“センサア”というのは？
English: What kind of sensors are you talking about?

Japanese: 地下倉庫には、ドア以外にも 出入りできる場所がありました。
English: There are other places in the basement that someone could enter and exit from.

Japanese: エアア・コンディショナアのダクト に排水口、ネコ用のドアなどなど。
English: There are air conditioner ducts, sewer pipes... And a cat door as well.

Japanese: すべてに熱感知・赤外線・紫外線・ その他モロモロのセンサアをッ！
English: I hooked up heat-detecting, infrared and ultra-violet sensors to each of them.

Japanese: それは‥‥ご自分で用意された のですか？
English: That's a lot of hardware... Was it all yours?

Japanese: 機材はすべて、高菱屋百貨店に 用意していただきました。
English: Lordly Tailor Department Store was kind enough to provide the monitoring hardware.

Japanese: もちろん‥‥この写真を 撮影した防犯キャメラも、ね。
English: Naturally, the security camera that took the photo belongs to them as well.

Japanese: （‥‥つまり、それらの機材に 細工はできなかったワケか‥‥）
English: (In other words, he couldn't have rigged the equipment, huh...?)

Japanese: クックックッ‥‥ウタガイは 晴れましたかな？　弁護士殿。
English: Hee hee hee hee hee... Has that cleared up any doubts you had about me, Sir Lawyer?

Japanese: そのコムピュウタも、高菱屋の モノだったんですか？
English: So did that computer belong to Lordly Tailor as well?

Japanese: さよう。‥‥まあ、デエタを 管理するプログラムは、
English: Correct. Well, except for the program that manages the data.

Japanese: この哀牙が、ホオムメエドの 味わいで作りあげたモノですがね！
English: That was specially designed by me, Luke Atmey!

Japanese: （それなら‥‥哀牙探偵は データを自由にイジれたワケだ）
English: (In that case, he could have easily manipulated the data.)

Japanese: クックックッ‥‥ どうかしましたかな？　弁護士殿。
English: Hee hee hee hee hee... What's wrong, Sir Lawyer?

Japanese: あの‥‥なんですか？ “たばかる”って。
English: Umm... What does that mean? "Discombobulated"?

Japanese: はあ‥‥最近のワカいヒトは これだからコマります。
English: Hmm... Young people these days... They really irritate me.

Japanese: うつくしく古いコトバは、 こうして失われていくのですね‥‥
English: They allow perfectly good old words to die until everyone forgets what they mean.

Japanese: スミマセン‥‥。で、 どういうイミなんですか？
English: Sorry, but what exactly DOES it mean anyway?

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥ド忘れしました。 なんでしたっけ？
English: Now I've forgotten. What was I saying?

Japanese: （最近のおジイさんも 似たようなもんじゃないか！）
English: (Jeez. It's better than old people who forget what they were saying 5 seconds ago!)

Japanese: ‥‥どうやら、結論が出たようだ。 証言をつづけて、かまわねえぜ。
English: ...Well, it looks like we've cleared that up. You can go on with your testimony.

Japanese: “かたわらの武器”というのは？
English: What do you mean by "weapon from the side"?

Japanese: にっくき怪人も、まさかこの哀牙が 現場にいようとは思わなかった！
English: Naturally, that thief had no idea that I, Luke Atmey, was hiding in the area!

Japanese: 倉庫へ通じる扉の近くに立っていた 女人像から、カタナをうばったッ！
English: He grabbed the sword from the statue that was standing by the door to the warehouse.

Japanese: カタナ‥‥って、くねくねと エダわかれしたヤツですか？
English: Sword...? You mean the sword that was all twisted like a tree branch?

Japanese: さよう。‥‥さいわい、刃は 立っていなかったようでしたが。
English: Correct. Fortunately for me, the blade was not sharp.

Japanese: （やはり‥‥七支刀のコトだな）
English: (OK, so he IS talking about the Shichishito...)

Japanese: 怪人は、カタナを取った。 それで、証人のほうは‥‥？
English: So the thief armed himself with a sword. And what about yourself, witness?

Japanese: ウデっぷしには 自信があったんですか？
English: You had that much faith in your own fighting abilities?

Japanese: さよう。我が中学生時代は、 拳闘倶楽部の副主将代理補佐ゆえ。
English: But of course. In college, I was the 2nd in-charge of the boxing club.

Japanese: ‥‥いまひとつ、スゴさが 伝わってきません。
English: I'm sorry if I fail to find that appropriately impressive.

Japanese: しかしヒキョウなるは、我が怪人 にしてライヴァルたる仮面マスク！
English: However! My opponent in the ring this time was my arch-nemesis, Mask☆DeMasque!

Japanese: （もうメチャクチャだな‥‥）
English: (This guy is a real piece of work...)

Japanese: そのときのコト、もう少し 詳しく聞かせてもらえますか？
English: Can you tell us a little more about what happened?

Japanese: カタナを持っている相手と闘うは、 ハアドでメロウ、そしてヘロウ。
English: My opponent was both powerful and vicious. You might say he was... "powericious".

Japanese: （なんだ“ヘロウ”って‥‥）
English: ("P-Powericious"?)

Japanese: トクイの戦法で相手しましたが‥‥ 一瞬、ヒカリに目がくらんだッ！
English: I assumed the "Atmey Fighting Stance", but a sudden flash of light blinded me!

Japanese: そこを、すかさずチェックメエト！ ‥‥やられたワケですな。
English: That of course was checkmate! My opponent had bested me.

Japanese: （どうする？　もう少し、 聞いてみるか‥‥？）
English: (What do I do now? Should I ask more about this...?)

Japanese: （とにかく、哀牙探偵がポカリと やられたのはジジツみたいだ）
English: (Well, I guess it's true that Atmey was knocked senseless after all.)

Japanese: （もっと、他のコトを聞いたほうが よさそうだな‥‥）
English: (I'd better ask him about something else instead...)

Japanese: その、目がくらんだ “ヒカリ”というのは‥‥？
English: So what was this flash of light that blinded you?

Japanese: ‥‥いわばアレは、ゴオルデン。 女人の像から発したモノでしょう。
English: ...I was bathed in a golden light that seemed to come from the statue of the woman.

Japanese: （綾里　供子の像、か‥‥）
English: (The statue of Ami Fey, I'm guessing...)

Japanese: あんまり参考にならなかったね。
English: Well that wasn't very much help at all...

Japanese: “トクイの戦法”というのは？
English: What is this "Atmey Fighting Style"?

Japanese: それこそは、探偵のシイクレット！ ‥‥と言いたいところですが‥‥
English: I'm sorry but that's a trade secret... I really can't say any more.

Japanese: よろしい。教えてしんぜよう。 みなさまも、明日から、ゼヒに。
English: But, I suppose I can tell you if I absolutely must.

Japanese: ‥‥カベを背に闘うのです。これで 背後を取られるコトはありませぬ。
English: The main thing is to put your back to the wall. That way no one can get behind you.

Japanese: （カベを‥‥背に？）
English: (That's it...? That's the "Atmey Fighting Style"?)

Japanese: ふむう‥‥いかがですか、弁護人。 この証言は‥‥？
English: Hmmm... Well, Mr. Wright? What about that testimony...?

Japanese: もちろん、重要です！ 探偵のシイクレットなワケですし。
English: Of course it's important! We've learned a detective's SECRET technique, after all.

Japanese: そうですな。夜道で襲われたとき、 私も参考にしましょう。
English: Yes, indeed... I'll remember to use it if I ever take a walk alone late at night.

Japanese: （裁判長‥‥なぜか、ぼくを ジッと見つめているぞ‥‥）
English: (Why the heck is he eye-balling me like that...? It's creeping me out...)

Japanese: ‥‥それでは、証人！
English: ...Now then, witness!

Japanese: 今のシイクレットを証言に 加えていただきましょう！
English: We'll go ahead and add that secret information to your official testimony!

Japanese: ‥‥あの。 マユアイ‥‥というのは？
English: Umm... Where is this "third eye" you're talking about?

Japanese: さよう‥‥右のマユと左のマユの ちょうど中央、ちょっと上あたり。
English: Oh... it's right between my right and left eyebrows.

Japanese: ‥‥ある種、ヒタイと言いかえても モンダイないでしょう。
English: ...It's quite invisible to the unenlightened. You may call it the forehead, if you like.

Japanese: （じゃあ、 最初からそっちで言えよ！）
English: (Why didn't you just say that in the first place!?)

Japanese: やあれ、弁護士殿！　マユアイに シワがよっておりますぞ！
English: Sir Lawyer! It looks like the creases in your own forehead are getting deeper!

Japanese: ‥‥あの探偵さん、ヒタイを 殴られたの？
English: So Mr. Atmey got hit on the forehead then?

Japanese: そう言ってるね。
English: That's what he says.

Japanese: ううん‥‥なんか気になるなあ。
English: Hmm... I've got a funny feeling about that.

Japanese: （‥‥じつは、ぼくもだ）
English: (Actually, so do I.)

Japanese: 哀牙さん！　あなたの証言は、 バラバラにしてガタガタです。
English: Detective Atmey! Your testimony is crumbling like a house of cards.

Japanese: さあれ、弁護士殿！ これはこれは、ユカイしごく。
English: What fun this is, Sir Lawyer! It is truly a pleasure to cross swords with you!

Japanese: この哀牙に、そのような ほほえましき挑戦状‥‥
English: And now, once again, you have thrown down the gauntlet at my armored feet...

Japanese: きのう、あなたは こう言っていたハズです。
English: I believe this is what you said yesterday.

Japanese: まっこと、ヒキョウなり 仮面マスク！
English: No, the coward must have wormed his way in through somewhere besides the door...

Japanese: この好敵手の後ろアタマを、 このような危険なもので殴るとは！
English: Then my arch-nemesis struck me on the head from behind with this gruesome item here!

Japanese: 後ろアタマ‥‥
English: From behind, huh?

Japanese: しかし今、あなたは証言した。 “ヒタイをコツンとやられた”‥‥
English: But just now you testified that he struck you on the forehead...

Japanese: もし本当に殴られたのなら、 まちがえるコトなど、あり得ない！
English: I hardly think you could forget where you were hit on the head!

Japanese: ‥‥うぐっ！　どうやら‥‥ やや、ウッカリしていたようです。
English: ...Urk! It seems... I-I've made another mistake.

Japanese: ‥‥哀牙さん。オカシイのは、 今の証言だけじゃありません。
English: ...Detective Atmey. That's not the only strange part of your testimony.

Japanese: それは‥‥ どういうイミですかな？
English: Wh-What do you mean by that?

Japanese: たとえば‥‥
English: For example...

Japanese: この予告状を、警察にかくして いたのが、すでに不自然です！
English: The very fact that you hid the calling card from the police itself is strange!

Japanese: まるで‥‥警察が警備にあたるのを 恐れていたかのように見えます！
English: It's almost as if... You were afraid they were going to help with security!

Japanese: く‥‥うむむ。
English: U-Urk...!

Japanese: 天才は、いつの世も理解されにくい ‥‥そういうコト、ですかな。
English: Geniuses such as myself have always been misunderstood! How sad...

Japanese: ちがいますよ！
English: That's wrong!

Japanese: ‥‥だれしも、まちがいはある。
English: ...To err is human, to forgive divine.

Japanese: 人間の行動は、リクツだけじゃ ワリ切れねえのさ。
English: Humans aren't machines... They have souls, feelings.

Japanese: だからこそ‥‥ヒトはヒトとして、 トキめき、キラめくんだぜ‥‥
English: They live, they die, they love, they hate... And yes, they even make mistakes...

Japanese: いやいやいや。これは、そんな キレイなハナシじゃない！
English: Hey, hold on! It-It's not as pretty as that!

Japanese: じゃあ、どんなハナシだ！
English: Really? What is it like then!?

Japanese: ギモンはトコトン追求する。 そいつが‥‥オレのルールだぜ。
English: Always chase a riddle down to the end. That's one of my rules.

Japanese: （‥‥こいつは‥‥）
English: (This is it...)

Japanese: （意外に早くめぐってきた、 反撃のチャンスかもしれない！）
English: (This might just be my chance to turn things around!)

Japanese: ‥‥成歩堂くん。あなたの 主張を聞かせてください。
English: ...Mr. Wright. What exactly is it that you're asserting?

Japanese: ‥‥わかりました。 弁護側の主張は‥‥！
English: Very well, Your Honor. The defense asserts that...

Japanese: この証人は、じつは‥‥ 名探偵などではないのです！
English: The truth is... this witness is no Ace Detective at all!

Japanese: ‥‥では‥‥いったい、 この哀牙は、なんだと？
English: ...So then what exactly are you saying that I am?

Japanese: ええと‥‥そうですね。 並の探偵、というか。
English: Uh, umm... Well, an average detective...?

Japanese: たいして、変わりませんな。
English: I hardly see the relevance of that.

Japanese: な、何やってんの、なるほどくん！ アイツを追いつめるんでしょ？
English: Wh-What are you doing, Nick!? Weren't you going to go on the attack?

Japanese: そのつもりだったけど‥‥ ぼくのほうが追いつめられてるな。
English: I was planning on it, but it looks I'm the one who got attacked instead.

Japanese: もっとマジメに考えるように！
English: Think harder next time!

Japanese: この証人は‥‥星威岳　哀牙の ニセモノなのですッ！
English: This witness isn't Luke Atmey... He's a fake!

Japanese: ‥‥仮面マスクにつづいて、 この哀牙まで、ニセモノだと‥‥？
English: ...So now it's not just Mask☆DeMasque. You're saying I'm a fake, too?

Japanese: 名探偵と言われる星威岳　哀牙が、 こんなトボけているはずがない！
English: An Ace Detective could never be as forgetful as this guy!

Japanese: アンタ自身がニセモノだから‥‥ そう見えるんだろうぜ。
English: Imposters like you are often suspicious of others as well.

Japanese: やれやれ。次は、この私が ニセモノ呼ばわりされそうですな。
English: Really, this is too much. Next you'll be calling me a fake, too!

Japanese: コタエはシンプルです！ すべてのナゾがスッキリする。
English: The answer is simple! It's all clear to me now!

Japanese: この星威岳　哀牙こそが‥‥ 怪人☆仮面マスクだったのです！
English: Detective Luke Atmey's true identity is actually... Mask☆DeMasque!

Japanese: うぐほほおおッ！
English: Yeeeeaaargggh!

Japanese: 静粛に！　静粛に！　静粛に！ 弁護人！　どういうことですか！
English: Order! Order in the court! Mr. Wright! What is the meaning of this!?

Japanese: ‥‥哀牙探偵には、 不自然な点が多すぎました。
English: There are too many unnatural parts to Mr. Atmey's story.

Japanese: 犯行現場では“かくれている” ため、だれも彼の姿を見ていない。
English: He says he was "hiding" at the crime scenes, which is why no one ever saw him there.

Japanese: それなのに、いつのまにか イトノコ刑事と警察を出し抜いて、
English: And then, in his last case, he manages to outperform Detective Gumshoe and...

Japanese: 前回の事件では、怪人の盗みだした エモノを、取り戻している‥‥
English: ...the entire police force to "miraculously" retrieve the stolen treasure.

Japanese: そ、それは‥‥
English: Th-That's because...

Japanese: 現場の状況や怪人の犯行データから その手口をカンペキにスイリして、
English: I analyzed the crime scene data and made an exquisitely elegant deduction.

Japanese: 警察の見落としているポイントに、 あらかじめ、先回りを‥‥
English: I picked up clues that the police overlooked in order to arrive at a...

Japanese: ちがうでしょう、哀牙探偵。 コタエは、もっとシンプルだった！
English: Oh, please! The explanation is far simpler than that, "Detective Atmey"!

Japanese: あなた自身が、 怪人☆仮面マスクだったのです。
English: The truth is that you are, in fact, Mask☆DeMasque.

Japanese: ぐはらあッ！
English: Gurk...!

Japanese: し‥‥しかし、弁護人！ この写真には‥‥怪人の姿が！
English: B-But Mr. Wright! Th-This photo! It clearly shows Mask☆DeMasque!

Japanese: これを撮影したカメラは、 高菱屋のものです！
English: This security camera belongs to Lordly Tailor Department Store!

Japanese: 細工など、できないハズでは‥‥
English: He shouldn't have been able to manipulate it...

Japanese: 細工なんて、必要ありません！
English: He didn't need to manipulate it!

Japanese: 星威岳　哀牙は、“探偵”として 地下倉庫を警備するフリをして‥‥
English: He gained access to the warehouse under the pretense of providing "security"...

Japanese: “怪人”として、ただ ツボを盗めばよかったのです！
English: Then he simply dressed as the "thief" and stole the urn.

Japanese: なんと‥‥名探偵が‥‥大怪盗！
English: So... the Ace Detective is actually an Ace Thief!

Japanese: そうなのですか！　証人ッ！
English: Is this true, witness!?
Aha...

Japanese: あーっはっはっはっはっはっはっ はっはっはっはっはっはっはっは！
English: Hee hee hee ha ha ha Aha ha ha ha ha ha ha! Ho ho ho hee hee hee!

Japanese: 仮面マスクの手口はまさに、天才！ 名探偵・星威岳　哀牙こそがッ！
English: DeMasque's M. O. is pure genius! And so am I, Luke Atmey, Ace Detective!!

Japanese: ‥‥その正体に、ふさわしいのかも しれませんな、みなみなさまがた！
English: You're very clever to have come to such a conclusion! I am impressed, Sir Lawyer!

Japanese: な‥‥なんですとォォ！
English: Wh-Whaaaaat!?

Japanese: まさか‥‥証人！ 認めるのですか‥‥ッ！
English: Witness! You... You're admitting it!?

Japanese: な‥‥なるほどくん！
English: N-Nick! Now's your chance!

Japanese: よし‥‥このまま、 一気にキメちゃおう！
English: Yes! Time to put the last nail in this guy's coffin!

Japanese: 星威岳　哀牙さん！ あなたを、怪人として‥‥
English: Detective Atmey! When you assumed the thief's identity--

Japanese: ゴドー・ブレンド１０２号。 ‥‥オレのオゴリだぜ。
English: Godot Blend #102... ...My personal favorite.

Japanese: ご‥‥ゴドー検事ッ！
English: M-Mr. Godot!

Japanese: 名探偵イコール、怪人‥‥ 絵空ゴトはキレイに見えるモンだ。
English: "The Ace Detective is actually an Ace Thief!" I smell a best-selling novel!

Japanese: だけどな‥‥。現実はそんなに、 キレイじゃねえのサ。
English: There's only one problem... It simply isn't true.

Japanese: し、しかし！ この弁護人の主張には、スジが‥‥
English: B-But Mr. Godot! Mr. Wright has made some very strong points and I...

Japanese: ‥‥たしかに、スジだらけで ゴリゴリしてるようだぜ。
English: I will admit my opponent has woven a compelling narrative out of whole cloth.

Japanese: どうやら‥‥おイタがすぎた みたいだな、弁護士さん‥‥
English: But it is, in fact, nothing more than a patchwork quilt. Mr. Trite...

Japanese: この探偵が、怪人。それなら‥‥ 証拠を見せてくれよ。
English: If this detective really is the thief, then show us the proof of your claim.

Japanese: そのコーヒーのようにカンペキで ‥‥そして、熱いヤツをッ！
English: But it had better be as hot... and as perfect as the coffee dripping down your face!

Japanese: 弁護人！　いつまでも ボーゼンとしていないで‥‥
English: Well, Mr. Wright!? Don't just stand there!

Japanese: 決定的な証拠を 提示してもらえますかな！
English: This court would like to see this decisive proof you have! Quickly!

Japanese: え！　そ、そうですね‥‥ （ちょっと、急ぎすぎたか‥‥？）
English: Huh!? Oh! Y-Yes, of course! (Wh-What's the big rush...?)

Japanese: だ、大丈夫なの、なるほどくん？
English: Are you alright, Nick?

Japanese: （‥‥哀牙探偵は今、 かなり弱っている‥‥）
English: (Atmey looks pretty rattled right now...)

Japanese: （できれば、ここで勝負を キメてしまいたい）
English: (I'd like to finish this right now if I can.)

Japanese: （それだけの証拠を‥‥ ぼくは、持っているのか？）
English: (But can I really do it?)

Japanese: 哀牙探偵こそが仮面マスクである という、決定的な証拠は‥‥！
English: The decisive evidence that proves Mr. Luke Atmey is in fact Mask☆DeMasque...

Japanese: ‥‥もちろん、弁護側には 決定的な証拠があります！
English: As usual, the defense comes prepared, Your Honor!

Japanese: わかりました。‥‥それでは、 提示していただきましょう。
English: Very well then... Let's see what you've got.

Japanese: この証人・星威岳　哀牙が、 仮面マスクであるという証拠を！
English: Please present proof that Mask☆DeMasque is actually Mr. Luke Atmey!

Japanese: これが‥‥その証拠ですッ！
English: Here's your proof!

Japanese: ‥‥いかがですか、ゴドー検事。
English: What do you think, Mr. Godot?

Japanese: どうやら‥‥弁護士さんはまだ、 目がさめてないらしいぜ。
English: It looks to me like Mr. Trite is still a mite sleepy.

Japanese: ゴドー・ブレンドをもうイッパイ、 いっとくかい？　‥‥弁護士さん。
English: Perhaps he'd like a big mug of my special Godot blend? Well?

Japanese: い、いえいえ。ケッコウです！
English: N-No thanks, I'm fine! Really!

Japanese: （どうする‥‥？　ここで判断を まちがうと、おしまいだ！）
English: (What do I do now? Another mistake like that and I'm through!)

Japanese: （決定的な証拠がないかぎり、 深追いはイノチ取りだけど‥‥）
English: (Without some decisive evidence, I could be risking everything...)

Japanese: 証拠は‥‥証拠は‥‥‥‥！ ‥‥まだ、ありません。
English: Proof? Of course I... I... ...I've got nothing.

Japanese: クッ‥‥！　そうだ。
English: Ha...! Just what I thought.

Japanese: オトコは、ミジメなときでも カオを上げなきゃいけねえ。
English: A man has to hold his head up high no matter how bad things get, after all.

Japanese: ‥‥うううう‥‥
English: Uuugh...

Japanese: ‥‥そうですか。
English: I see...

Japanese: 証拠さえあれば、“あるいは”と 思ったのですが‥‥
English: I thought perhaps you had some evidence to back up your assertion...

Japanese: （‥‥ダメだ！　ここで攻撃を やめてしまったら‥‥）
English: (This is no good! I've got to stay on the attack!)

Japanese: （二度とコイツを怪人として 告発するチャンスはなくなる！）
English: (I'll never get another chance to prove that this guy is the thief!)

Japanese: なるほどくん‥‥がんばって！ もう一度、考えようよ！
English: Don't give up, Nick! Think harder and try again!

Japanese: ‥‥ダメだ‥‥ やっぱり‥‥まだ、早すぎたんだ。
English: It's no good... I'm just not ready yet...

Japanese: そ、そんな‥‥！ あたしたち、負けちゃうの‥‥？
English: B-But... Are you going to just give up and let us lose this?

Japanese: ‥‥どうやら、弁護士殿。 ツメをあやまったようですな‥‥
English: So, you've come to your senses, have you Sir Lawyer?

Japanese: う‥‥むむむ‥‥ （い、言い返せない‥‥）
English: I... Unnngh... (Argh! I can't think of a counter-attack at all...)

Japanese: この証人のウタガイは、これで 晴れたようです。
English: It seems the cloud of suspicion surrounding this witness has lifted.

Japanese: それでは、ゴドー検事。 まだ他に、何か証言を‥‥
English: Mr. Godot. If you have anything further to add, then--

Japanese: あ‥‥あ‥‥あなたは‥‥
English: Wh... Wh-Who...

Japanese: ‥‥ダレですか？
English: ...Who are you?

Japanese: どうでもいいでしょ、 そんなの。
English: That doesn't really matter right now, does it?

Japanese: ま、まれかさん！ ‥‥どうして、ここへ！
English: Ms. DeLite! What are you doing here?

Japanese: リューイチくん！
English: Nicky boy!

Japanese: アナタたちが探してるの‥‥ コレじゃないかな！
English: The thing you've been looking for... I think I found it!

Japanese: それは‥‥カバン、ですか。
English: You mean... that bag!?

Japanese: 問題は、このカバンの中身よ。 ‥‥どう？
English: No, not the bag! What's in the bag! ...Well?

Japanese: そ‥‥それは‥‥
English: Th-That's...

Japanese: 《倉院のツボ》だよッ！ なるほどくん！
English: The Sacred Urn!! Nick! It's the urn!

Japanese: 静粛に！　静粛に！　静粛に！ ‥‥あなた！　おじょうさん！
English: Order! Order! Order! ...You! Madame...!

Japanese: そのツボ‥‥いったい、 どこで見つけたのですかッ！
English: That urn... Where did you find it!?

Japanese: 聞いておどろかないでよ。
English: You'll never believe it...

Japanese: あのエラそうな名探偵、 星威岳　哀牙サンの事務所よっ！
English: It was in the office of Mr. Fancy Pants Ace Detective! ...Luke Atmey!

Japanese: （まれかちゃーん、サイコーだあ）
English: Oh, Dessie! You're the best!

Japanese: 証拠品《倉院のツボ》の データを書きなおした。
English: Sacred Urn updated in the Court Record.

Japanese: ‥‥さあ！ いかがですか、哀牙さん！
English: Well? How do you explain that one, Mr. Atmey!?

Japanese: 今度こそ、言い逃れはできない！ あなたこそが‥‥
English: Even you are going to have a hard time weaseling out of this one!

Japanese: クッ‥‥！　なさけねえ‥‥
English: Ha...! Pathetic...

Japanese: ご、ゴドー検事‥‥？
English: Mr. Godot...? Do you have something you wish to say?

Japanese: ムカシ、男は背中で語ったもんだ。 それが、今じゃあ‥‥
English: Yes, Your Honor... It simply amazes me how quickly times change...

Japanese: 男のコトバを受け止められるヤツは いねえ。これも時代だな‥‥
English: In the old days, a man was to be taken at his word. It's truly sad...

Japanese: ま、まさか‥‥ まだ認めないつもりですか！
English: You're still denying that Mr. Atmey was involved!?

Japanese: モンダイは、ツボを持ってきた ネエちゃんだ。
English: Before casting aspersions at Detective Atmey, consider the young lady here.

Japanese: アンタ‥‥たしか、 天杉　優作のカミさん、だったな。
English: Your name is Desirée... Desirée DeLite. Is that correct?

Japanese: だ、だから？　それがなんなの？
English: Y-Yes! What about it?

Japanese: クッ‥‥！　泣かせるじゃねえか。
English: Ha...! How charming...

Japanese: ダンナを助けるために‥‥ こんなコトまでするとはよォ！
English: The lengths that a woman is willing to go to save her husband is truly inspiring.

Japanese: な‥‥何よ、それ！
English: Wh-What are you insinuating!?

Japanese: 犯人のカミさんなら、どこからでも ツボを“発見”できる。
English: As the wife of the criminal, you could have "discovered" that stolen urn anywhere.

Japanese: モチロン、 探偵の事務所からでも‥‥なァ。
English: Including the office of the good detective here.

Japanese: ツボは見つかった。 ‥‥だからなんだ？
English: So you found the urn. What does that prove?

Japanese: モンダイは‥‥ソイツがどこに あったのか‥‥だぜ。
English: It certainly doesn't prove where the urn was before you "found" it.

Japanese: このツボ‥‥アイツの事務所から 持ってきたのよ！
English: What...? I just brought it here from the detective's office!

Japanese: ‥‥この街には‥‥ 悲しいウソがあふれてやがる。
English: Please Madame... This town is already filled to the brim with lies.

Japanese: もう、これ以上は必要ねえぜ。 ‥‥おネエちゃん。
English: Any more could only compound the tragedy we have been witness to.

Japanese: ちがう！　アタシ‥‥そんな 汚いコト、していない！
English: You're wrong! I would never...! I would never do such a thing!

Japanese: ま、まれかさん‥‥
English: Ms. DeLite...

Japanese: 助けて！　リューイチくんなら ‥‥証明してくれるよね！
English: Please, Nicky boy! You've got to help me talk some sense into these people!

Japanese: （何か‥‥ 方法があるに、ちがいない！）
English: (There must be some way!)

Japanese: （あのツボが、哀牙探偵事務所に あったことを立証するには‥‥）
English: (I've got to prove that urn was actually in the Atmey Detective Agency...)

Japanese: どうした？　しゃべるのが シゴトじゃなかったのか‥‥？
English: What's wrong? Isn't it your job to come up with something to say...?

Japanese: なるほどくん！ 立証なんて、カンタンじゃない！
English: Nick! Come on! It should be easy to prove!

Japanese: だって‥‥あたしたち、 調べたんだよ。あのバッグ！
English: After all, we checked out that bag, didn't we?

Japanese: ‥‥ああ。 たしかに、おぼえてるよ。
English: You're right. I remember doing that.

Japanese: 中に入っていたのは‥‥カタくて、 丸くて‥‥つるつるしていた。
English: Whatever was in there, it was hard and round... And smooth, too.

Japanese: さわった感じ、どう考えても ツボじゃないの、それ！
English: Yes! It must have been the urn! It must have!

Japanese: でも‥‥ぼくは それを、見ていないんだ。
English: But... I never actually saw it with my own eyes.

Japanese: 見ようとしたところで、 哀牙が帰ってきた。だから‥‥
English: Atmey walked in just when I was about to have a look at it...

Japanese: 中身が本当にツボだったか‥‥ ぼくたちには証言できない。
English: So even if it was the urn in there, I can't testify that it was.

Japanese: そんなああッ！
English: Ack! You're right!!

Japanese: クッ‥‥！ 見なおしたぜ、まるほどう。
English: Ha...! It looks like I was wrong about you, Mr. Trite.

Japanese: ウソにあふれた街で‥‥今、 小さな真実の灯がともりやがった。
English: In this town overflowing with lies, there's still a single flickering candle of truth.

Japanese: ‥‥リューイチくん‥‥！
English: Nicky boy...!

Japanese: （たしかにぼくは、あのツボを 見てはいない‥‥）
English: (Well, it's true that I never actually saw the urn...)

Japanese: （だけど‥‥他の方向から、 立証できるはずだ‥‥！）
English: (But maybe I can still testify about it from another angle!)

Japanese: ‥‥いかがですかな、弁護人。 あきらめますか‥‥？
English: What about it, Mr. Wright? Have you given up yet...?

Japanese: ‥‥今は‥‥ まだ、立証はできません。
English: I can't prove anything yet.

Japanese: なるほどくん！
English: But Nick!

Japanese: ‥‥どうやら‥‥ ここまでのようですな。
English: Very well... It looks like that's all then.

Japanese: そう‥‥このツボこそ、 決定的な証拠だからな‥‥。
English: Exactly... And this urn is all the proof I need to make my case...

Japanese: 決定的な‥‥証拠？
English: What?

Japanese: まだ、わからねえみたいだな。 ‥‥いいか、まるほどう。
English: You still don't get it, huh? Listen closely, Mr. Trite.

Japanese: このツボは、怪人☆仮面マスクが 盗んだモノだ。
English: This is the urn that Mask☆DeMasque stole, right?

Japanese: それを今、被告人のカミさんが 持って、ノコノコ出てきたんだ。
English: Now the wife of the accused comes strolling in here with the very same urn, correct?

Japanese: さあて‥‥怪人は、ダレだ？
English: Now, who would you think is the thief?

Japanese: あ‥‥っ！
ダメ！　アタシ、 そんなつもりじゃあ‥‥
English: No! That's wrong! That's not why I've got it...

Japanese: な‥‥なるほどくん！ ダメだよ！　コレじゃあ‥‥
English: N-Nick! Y-You can't let him do this...

Japanese: （たしかに‥‥ここはもう、 １つしか方法はない！）
English: (Hmm, this isn't good... There has to be another way around this...)

Japanese: 裁判長！　もう一度だけ‥‥ 考えさせてくださいッ！
English: Your Honor! Please let me think it over one more time!

Japanese: ‥‥弁護人。あなたもそろそろ おぼえてもいいころです。
English: Mr. Wright. It's high time that you learn your lesson.

Japanese: いいかげんな発言は、 イノチ取りになるということを！
English: The courtroom is no place for poorly thought-out or rash declarations!

Japanese: （今度こそ‥‥シッパイは ユルされないぞ‥‥）
English: (I have to be careful... He won't be so nice next time...)

Japanese: そのツボが、どこにあったか‥‥ 立証することは、可能です。
English: I can prove where the urn was...

Japanese: ‥‥ツボに残った “指紋”によって！
English: ...by the fingerprints on it!

Japanese: 指紋‥‥ですか‥‥
English: Fingerprints, eh...

Japanese: あいやッ！
English: Oh come now!

Japanese: あいや、弁護士殿。これぞ笑止！ よりによって、指紋とは！
English: Now you're really making me laugh, Sir Lawyer! Fingerprints indeed!

Japanese: よいですかな？
English: May I go on? Good.

Japanese: ツボに、この哀牙の指紋が残って いるのは、しごくトーゼンですぞ。
English: Now, it would be perfectly understandable if my fingerprints were on the urn.

Japanese: なにしろ、ツボを警備していた のは、この哀牙なのです！
English: After all, it was I who was guarding the urn in the first place!

Japanese: そもそも、我はこうして、 手袋をハメておる。
English: In any case, I am always in the habit of wearing gloves as you can see.

Japanese: ザンネンながら、我が指紋は、 証拠にはなりますまい。
English: So unfortunately, my fingerprints wouldn't be evidence of anything.

Japanese: ‥‥いかがですかな？　この証人の 指紋は、イミがないようですが。
English: What about it, Mr. Wright? This witness's fingerprints would mean nothing anyway.

Japanese: ど‥‥どうするの？ なるほどくん！
English: N-Nick, what are you going to do now?

Japanese: （ここまで来て‥‥ あとには引けない！）
English: (I've come too far to turn back now!)

Japanese: （哀牙探偵が、あのツボを事務所に 持ちこんだのは、きのう‥‥）
English: (Atmey must have brought the urn back to his office yesterday...)

Japanese: （そこで指紋をつけた人物が ひとり、いるじゃないか‥‥！）
English: (And there, I'm sure someone must have left their fingerprints on it...!)

Japanese: ツボには‥‥この人物の指紋が 残っているはずです！
English: The defense proposes that the fingerprints of this person should be on the urn!

Japanese: ‥‥いいコトを教えてやるよ。
English: Let me give you an important piece of advice.

Japanese: ゲームってのは‥‥最後まで 冷静だったほうが勝つんだぜ。
English: The one who keeps a cool head all the way until the game's end is the winner.

Japanese: もっといいコトを教えて あげましょう、成歩堂くん。
English: Let me give you an even more important piece of advice, Mr. Wright...

Japanese: ‥‥少なくとも、そのコタエでは 勝てませんぜ。
English: You are not going to win this game with that answer.

Japanese: なな、なんか裁判長まで カッコよくなってきたよ‥‥
English: Wow... Even the judge is making cool, snappy remarks now...

Japanese: （‥‥ぼくが まちがっていたのか‥‥？）
English: (D-Does that mean I was wrong again?)

Japanese: （ツボは‥‥きのう、あのバッグに 入れられて、事務所に置かれた）
English: (Hmm... The urn was put in that bag and carried to the detective's office yesterday.)

Japanese: （だから、ぼくが 探すべきなのは‥‥）
English: (That means the person I should be fingering is...)

Japanese: （きのう、あの事務所で ツボに指紋をつけた人物‥‥！）
English: (...the person who left their prints on the urn yesterday at the Atmey Detective Agency!)

Japanese: そんなヒト‥‥ホントに いるのかなあ‥‥？
English: Do you really think there's someone that could fit the bill?

Japanese: ‥‥さて。 どうしますかな？　弁護人。
English: Well, Mr. Wright? What have you decided to do?

Japanese: すみません‥‥もう一度、 最初から考えなおしてみます。
English: Forgive me, Your Honor. I've decided to think the whole thing over again.

Japanese: ‥‥さて。 どうしますかな？　弁護人。
English: Well, Mr. Wright? What have you decided?

Japanese: 裁判長！　もう一度だけ、 おねがいします。
English: Your Honor! Please give me just one more chance!

Japanese: そこまで！
English: That's enough!

Japanese: 本法廷は、これ以上の審議の 必要性を認めません。
English: This court sees no reason to further prolong this trial.

Japanese: さらに、被告に対する判決を 決める時間も、必要としません。
English: Nor is there any need for more time to decide the case against the defendant.

Japanese: これは、きわめて明白な事件です。 疑問の余地はない！
English: This case is extremely clear. I see no room for mis- interpretation of the facts.

Japanese: 被告人、天杉　優作に たった今、判決を言いわたします！
English: This court finds the defendant, Ron DeLite...

Japanese: 被告人はこれより身柄を 司直に預けるものとし、
English: The accused will surrender to the court immediately...

Japanese: １ヶ月以内に、高等裁判所において 通常裁判を受けることとします。
English: ...to be held pending trial at a higher court within a month from today's date.

Japanese: では、本日はこれにて閉廷！
English: That is all. The court is adjourned!
